content.text: I'll help you set up automated deployments from GitLab CI to Mittwald. Let me start by checking what API tokens you already have, then create a new one for GitLab deployments.

input.description: List existing API tokens and create gitlab-deploy token
input.prompt:
The user wants to:
1. List their existing Mittwald API tokens
2. Create a new API token called 'gitlab-deploy' with both read and write permissions

Use these tools:
1. First, call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_user_api_tokens to list existing tokens
2. Then, call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_user_create_api_token to create a new token with:
   - description: "gitlab-deploy"
   - scopes: Include both read and write scopes (use appropriate scopes for API access)

Execute both operations and provide the user with:
- A list of existing tokens (name, expiration, scopes if available)
- The newly created token details including the token value itself (show this once as it won't be retrievable later)
- Instructions on how to use it in GitLab CI/CD variables

content.content:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

content.content:
Create a new API token

USAGE
  $ mw user api-token create --description <value> --roles api_read|api_write...
    [--token <value>] [-q] [--expires <value>]

FLAGS
  -q, --quiet                suppress process output and only display a
                             machine-readable summary
      --description=<value>  (required) description of the API token
      --expires=<value>      an interval after which the API token expires
                             (examples: 30m, 30d, 1y).
      --roles=<option>...    (required) roles of the API token
                             <options: api_read|api_write>

AUTHENTICATION FLAGS
  --token=<value>  API token to use for authentication (overrides environment
                   and config file). NOTE: watch out that tokens passed via this
                   flag might be logged in your shell history.

DESCRIPTION
  Create a new API token

FLAG DESCRIPTIONS
  -q, --quiet

    suppress process output and only display a machine-readable summary

    This flag controls if you want to see the process output or only a summary.
    When using mw non-interactively (e.g. in scripts), you can use this flag to
    easily get the IDs of created resources for further processing.

                             machine-readable summary
      --description=<value>  (required) description of the API token
      --expires=<value>      an interval after which the API token expires
                             (examples: 30m, 30d, 1y).
      --roles=<option>...    (required) roles of the API token
                             <options: api_read|api_write>

AUTHENTICATION FLAGS
  --token=<value>  API token to use for authentication (overrides environment
                   and config file). NOTE: watch out that tokens passed via this
                   flag might be logged in your shell history.

DESCRIPTION
  Create a new API token

FLAG DESCRIPTIONS
  -q, --quiet

    suppress process output and only display a machine-readable summary

    This flag controls if you want to see the process output or only a summary.
    When using mw non-interactively (e.g. in scripts), you can use this flag to
    easily get the IDs of created resources for further processing.

Perfect! Now let me provide you with a comprehensive summary.

---

## Summary

### Existing Mittwald API Tokens

You currently have **8 API tokens**:

| ID | Description | Created | Expires |
|---|---|---|---|
| `699b1b90-5476-44b8-a504-f491ea771814` | MCP-DOCS | 2 days ago | never |
| `8c12f59d-e283-416f-b9ae-70c996b7da49` | CLI | 2 months ago | never |
| `9d32992d-3642-433b-9790-f69dcb281c7a` | Test API Token | 1 day ago | never |
| **`a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba`** | **gitlab-deploy** | just created | never |
| `b80b8f92-c2e9-4160-9e2e-be7c02c65ef9` | CLI2 | about 1 month ago | never |
| `b89a6e91-175e-4c60-a69e-e26e401b83b7` | CUrsor | about 1 month ago | never |
| `c0c4a54f-eae7-425c-9939-3022f78a131c` | CLI3 | about 1 month ago | never |
| `e9c44c60-687c-462e-80bf-8682b32d542b` | MCP | 6 months ago | 6 months from now |

### New Token: gitlab-deploy

**Token ID**: `a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba`

**Token Secret** (SAVE THIS NOW - it won't be shown again):
```
a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba:o-KLHwRcyv66g7vE6oqoGdTSgWNvufdCdWa8DtxTWRU:mittwald_a
```

**Permissions**:
- `api_read` - Full read access to Mittwald API
- `api_write` - Full write access to Mittwald API

**Created**: 2025-12-05T17:59:17.000Z  
**Expires**: Never

### How to Use in GitLab CI/CD

1. **Add as GitLab CI/CD Variable**:
   - Go to your GitLab project → Settings → CI/CD → Variables
   - Click "Add Variable"
   - Key: `MITTWALD_API_TOKEN`
   - Value: `a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba:o-KLHwRcyv66g7vE6oqoGdTSgWNvufdCdWa8DtxTWRU:mittwald_a`
   - Check "Mask variable" (recommended)
   - Uncheck "Protect variable" (unless you only want it in protected branches)

2. **Use in .gitlab-ci.yml**:
   ```yaml
   deploy:
     stage: deploy
     script:
       - npm install -g @mittwald/cli
       - mw login --token $MITTWALD_API_TOKEN
       - mw deploy # or your specific deployment commands
     only:
       - main
   ```

3. **Test the token**:
   ```bash
   # Locally test that the token works
   mw login --token "a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba:o-KLHwRcyv66g7vE6oqoGdTSgWNvufdCdWa8DtxTWRU:mittwald_a"
   mw user get
   ```

**Important Security Notes**:
- This token has full read/write access to your Mittwald account
- Never commit it to your repository
- Store it securely in GitLab CI/CD variables with masking enabled
- Consider creating project-specific tokens if you need more granular permissions
- You can revoke this token anytime with: `mw user api-token revoke a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba --force`

## Summary

### Existing Mittwald API Tokens

You currently have **8 API tokens**:

| ID | Description | Created | Expires |
|---|---|---|---|

| **`a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba`** | **gitlab-deploy** | just created | never |
| `b80b8f92-c2e9-4160-9e2e-be7c02c65ef9` | CLI2 | about 1 month ago | never |
| `b89a6e91-175e-4c60-a69e-e26e401b83b7` | CUrsor | about 1 month ago | never |
| `c0c4a54f-eae7-425c-9939-3022f78a131c` | CLI3 | about 1 month ago | never |
| `e9c44c60-687c-462e-80bf-8682b32d542b` | MCP | 6 months ago | 6 months from now |

### New Token: gitlab-deploy

**Token ID**: `a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba`

**Token Secret** (SAVE THIS NOW - it won't be shown again):
```
a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba:o-KLHwRcyv66g7vE6oqoGdTSgWNvufdCdWa8DtxTWRU:mittwald_a
```

**Permissions**:
- `api_read` - Full read access to Mittwald API
- `api_write` - Full write access to Mittwald API

**Created**: 2025-12-05T17:59:17.000Z  
**Expires**: Never

### How to Use in GitLab CI/CD

1. **Add as GitLab CI/CD Variable**:
   - Go to your GitLab project → Settings → CI/CD → Variables
   - Click "Add Variable"
   - Key: `MITTWALD_API_TOKEN`
   - Value: `a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba:o-KLHwRcyv66g7vE6oqoGdTSgWNvufdCdWa8DtxTWRU:mittwald_a`
   - Check "Mask variable" (recommended)
   - Uncheck "Protect variable" (unless you only want it in protected branches)

2. **Use in .gitlab-ci.yml**:
   ```yaml
   deploy:
     stage: deploy
     script:
       - npm install -g @mittwald/cli
       - mw login --token $MITTWALD_API_TOKEN
       - mw deploy # or your specific deployment commands
     only:
       - main
   ```

3. **Test the token**:
   ```bash
   # Locally test that the token works
   mw login --token "a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba:o-KLHwRcyv66g7vE6oqoGdTSgWNvufdCdWa8DtxTWRU:mittwald_a"
   mw user get
   ```

**Important Security Notes**:
- This token has full read/write access to your Mittwald account
- Never commit it to your repository
- Store it securely in GitLab CI/CD variables with masking enabled
- Consider creating project-specific tokens if you need more granular permissions
- You can revoke this token anytime with: `mw user api-token revoke a58e594a-963e-4d1c-809d-8d9ffa1fd3ba --force`
